rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO FEDERAL CREDIT UNION VS. DANIEL A. MALLEN et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 27, 2025, Line 1, PLAINTIFF SAN FRANCISCO FEDERAL CREDIT UNION's HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. Hearing required. Mr. Mallen is required to bring to the hearing proof that he paid his student loan payments the past three months. If Mr. Mallen appears at the hearing by Zoom, he is required to send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to counsel for plaintiff SFFCU attaching documents showing that he paid his student loan payments the past three months. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HK).

rulings.Case Title: OPTRICS INC ET AL VS. GRACE R. NEIBARON, ESQ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 27, 2025, Line 3, DEFENDANT CLOUD9 DISCOVERY LLC's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant Cloud9's demurrer to the fourth cause of action for breach of contract in the third amended complaint is overruled. Cloud9's prior demurrer based on CCP Section 430.10(g) was overruled and may not be re-asserted. Cloud9's argument that new circumstances mandate a reconsideration of this argument is unpersuasive given that plaintiff Optrics' new allegations have nothing to do with the basis for the prior ruling. In paragraph105 of the TAC plaintiff added the allegation that "Terreri and/or Neibaron were unfamiliar with electronic discovery matters, and were therefore unable to negotiate on Optrics' behalf with BNI's counsel" (TAC, Para 105.) This is sufficient to meet the intent standard outlined in Goonewardene for a third-party beneficiary theory because it shows that Cloud9 and Terreri and/or Neibaron entered into this agreement intending to benefit Plaintiff. The new allegation necessarily implies the contract was only created once Defendants Terreri and/or Neibaron realized they were insufficiently competent regarding the e-discovery in the underlying lawsuit. Defendant's assertion that the TAC does not properly allege other elements of a breach of contract claim also lack merit. The allegations in the TAC sufficiently allege a breach of contract claim against Cloud9. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HK).

rulings.Case Title: ADAM O'CONNOR VS. SCOTT NEWLIN JR. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Monetary And Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Jan-27-2025, Line 4, DEFENDANT AMERICAN MEDICAL RESPONSE WEST'S (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS "AMERICAN MEDICAL RESPONSE AMBULANCE SERVICE, INC.") Motion For Monetary And Terminating Sanctions. Defendant's motion for terminating sanctions is denied in its entirety. "A trial court must be cautious when imposing a terminating sanction because the sanction eliminates a party's fundamental right to trial, thus implicating due process rights." (Lopez v. Watchtower Bible and Tract Society of New York, Inc. (2016) 246 Cal.App.4th 566, 604.) There is no basis for a terminating or evidentiary sanction since plaintiff cured the cited non-compliance. While Mr. Buckley's declaration fails to comply with CCP 2015.5, there is no dispute that plaintiff's counsel ultimately paid the sanctions that were required pursuant to this court's September 17, 2024, order. (Reply, 2:5 ["Plaintiff would not have paid the sanctions but for this motion."].) While plaintiff should have paid the previously ordered monetary sanctions prior to this motion being filed, defendant's effort to leverage that non-payment into grounds to terminate this case lacked substantial justification, warranting the denial of monetary sanctions to defendant on this motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: CHANG H. AHN ET AL VS. BUCHALTER, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Re: Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 27, 2025, Line 10, DEFENDANT BUCHALTER's Hearing Re: Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. Defendant/petitioner Buchalter's petition to confirm arbitration award is granted. Although those papers were not accepted for filing, the court reviewed the papers that defendant/respondent HR E&I, Co. Ltd. attempted to file on or about January 9, 2025 opposing the confirmation of the arbitration award and seeking to vacate that award and considered those papers as if they had been timely filed. Buchalter has shown that the arbitration award should be confirmed and HR E&I failed to substantiate any grounds to oppose the confirmation, modify, or vacate the arbitration award. rulings.Case Title: SURESH KUMAR MYNAM VS. GAVIN NEWSOM
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 27, 2025, Line 5, DEFENDANT GAVIN NEWSOM IN HIS OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendant Gavin Newsom's demurrer to the complaint is sustained with leave to amend. No opposition filed. If plaintiff Suresh Kumar Mynam wishes to file a first amended complaint, he must do so no later than February 26, 2025 and must allege ultimate facts supporting at least one viable cause of action. In deciding whether to file a first amended complaint, the plaintiff should keep in mind that the Governor is not involved in the setting of San Francisco towing charges and likely lacks the authority to make changes to those charges. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: SAMANTHA PRINCE VS. MARSHALL BEHRMAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 27, 2025, Line 9, DEFENDANT MARSHALL BEHRMAN, SHARLENE BEHRMAN's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendants Marshall and Sharlene Behrman's demurrer to the sixth cause of action for conversion in plaintiff Samantha Prince's complaint is overruled. Ms. Prince alleges that her parents took her personal items, such as jewelry, clothing, and a fur coat, and destroyed them or refused to return them. These allegations are sufficient to overcome a pleading challenge to a conversion claim. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person.
